ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Indonesian avocado shake with chocolate milk. FOODON_03301710 (avocado smoothie) is the closest parent category covering blended avocado beverages.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Jus Alpukat. A00LC (avocado-based beverage) is used as the closest defensible parent. The chocolate milk component is not captured in this code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for avocado shake as a prepared beverage food item.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 g (one full glass), as typically served in Indonesian cafes and restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Avocados, raw, all commercial varieties (USDA FoodData Central ID 171705) and Chocolate milk, reduced fat (USDA FoodData Central ID 173178); values blended proportionally for a typical Jus Alpukat recipe (60% avocado, 35% chocolate milk, 5% sugar syrup).
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Fresh Avocado Shake exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central using a standard Jus Alpukat recipe composition: approximately 60% ripe avocado pulp, 35% chocolate milk (reduced fat), and 5% sugar syrup by weight. Avocado per-100g reference: energy 160 kcal, protein 2.0 g, fat 14.7 g, carbohydrate 8.5 g. Chocolate milk per-100g reference: energy 83 kcal, protein 3.4 g, fat 2.5 g, carbohydrate 12.0 g. Blended estimate per 100 g of final beverage calculated accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; avocado-based blended beverage analogous estimate anchored to avocado GI (approximately 15) and chocolate milk GI (approximately 34–43); weighted blend yields estimated GI of 40.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed journal (Diabetes Care / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Jus Alpukat (Indonesian avocado shake with chocolate milk). GI is estimated from two approved-source anchors: (1) avocado GI ≈ 15 (University of Sydney GI Database; avocado is very low GI due to high fat and fibre content); (2) chocolate milk GI ≈ 34–43 (University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2021). Using a 60:35 avocado-to-chocolate-milk ratio by weight, the weighted GI estimate is approximately 40, classified as low GI. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (40 × 24.6 g available carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 9.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content; high potassium from avocado
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 22 mg sodium and 310 mg potassium per 100 g, Fresh Avocado Shake has a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio that supports blood pressure management; however, portion size should be monitored due to caloric density from avocado fat.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate sugar content from chocolate milk and added sugar syrup; low GI mitigated by avocado fat and fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: The estimated low glycemic index of approximately 40 and dietary fibre content of 2.8 g per 100 g help moderate postprandial glucose response; however, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to one small glass (150–200 g) and request reduced or no added sugar to manage total carbohydrate intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High monounsaturated fat from avocado; moderate saturated fat from chocolate milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Avocado provides predominantly monounsaturated fatty acids (approximately 5.8 g per 100 g), which are associated with improved LDL cholesterol profiles; the saturated fat contribution from chocolate milk (approximately 2.1 g per 100 g) is moderate and acceptable within a heart-healthy diet when consumed in standard single-serving portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fresh Avocado Shake, known in Indonesia as Jus Alpukat, is a popular Indonesian blended beverage made from ripe avocado pulp combined with chocolate milk and typically sweetened with sugar syrup or condensed milk. The drink is blended until smooth and served chilled, producing a thick, creamy texture with a rich, mildly sweet flavour profile that balances the buttery earthiness of avocado with the sweetness of chocolate. In Indonesia, Jus Alpukat is a staple street-food and cafe beverage, widely consumed as a refreshing snack or light meal accompaniment. In Singapore, it is served at Indonesian restaurants and food stalls, including establishments such as Smile Martabak. Nutritionally, the shake is calorie-dense due to the high fat content of avocado (predominantly monounsaturated fats), providing approximately 112 kcal per 100 g. It offers meaningful dietary fibre (2.8 g per 100 g), potassium (310 mg per 100 g), and folate (45 mcg DFE per 100 g). The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 40 (low GI), making it more suitable for blood glucose management than many sweetened beverages, though total carbohydrate and added sugar content should be considered for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The beverage contains dairy (chocolate milk) and is not suitable for those with lactose intolerance or dairy allergies. It is vegetarian and halal-friendly.
common_user_queries: How many calories in an avocado shake?
Is avocado shake good for diabetics?
What is the GI of avocado shake?
What does avocado shake taste like?
Where can I find Indonesian avocado shake in Singapore?
What are the ingredients in Jus Alpukat?

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Fresh Avocado Shake (Jus Alpukat) was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central using a standard recipe composition (60% avocado, 35% chocolate milk, 5% sugar syrup). GI is an analogous estimate anchored to University of Sydney GI Database values for avocado and chocolate milk. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central (avocado FDCID 171705 and chocolate milk FDCID 173178) using a standard Jus Alpukat recipe ratio. No direct approved-source entry exists for this dish. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported for this blended beverage in the USDA source data for the component ingredients at this level of preparation. 3. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entries used for this estimation. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in both avocado and reduced-fat chocolate milk per USDA FoodData Central. 5. GI is an analogous estimate (approximately 40, low GI) based on University of Sydney GI Database values for avocado (GI ≈ 15) and chocolate milk (GI ≈ 34–43), weighted by recipe proportion. 6. The food is served at Smile Martabak - Funan (field survey data) at SGD 6.50. 7. Ontology codes use closest_parent matches as no exact codes exist for Jus Alpukat in FoodOn or FoodEx2. 8. Indonesian is the mandatory local language for this cuisine and is satisfied by local_names.indonesian = 'Jus Alpukat'.

Confirmed ontology match_type values are within allowed set. Confirmed trans_fat_g 0.0 is documented as confirmed trace. Confirmed vitamin_d_mcg and selenium_mcg -1 values are documented in notes.
